title: Ritsuko knows too damned much split
display_title: Ritsuko knows too damned much split
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 29, 2009 12:03 am Zaque wrote: BTW, we don't know who it was that worked with her after her "birth" do we?

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri May 29, 2009 6:24 am The idea that Ritsuko is a Jaquelin of all trades is pretty ridiculous from a scientific POV, as well. How she can be an expert computer programmer, engineer, biologist, and physician all in one goes a bit beyond the realm of "suspension of disbelief," even for a show where there are 300 foot tall cyborgs and the human soul can be held in a petri dish. Who knows, maybe Ritsuko herself was some kind of experiment...

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Fri May 29, 2009 8:16 am I always assumed because Rei was extra-super-special, she got attention from the top scientist. Also, the computers are supposed to be bio-mechanical, right? And the Evas have some technological links to the Magi, no? So I just assumed Ritsuko would be pretty much "in the know" about all these weird things. Presumably she's a genius of some sort, right? A polymath, perchance? Since everything is linked to begin with, her talents be spread around. Polymaths aren't that uncommon. You wouldn't want Researcher #7 working on the cloned-mom-seed-angel-whatever girl. Hehe. You'd want Ritsuko, I'd reckon.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri May 29, 2009 9:05 am Sure you would, as well as #8, #9, #10 and so on... replacing them as necessary and discarding the prior employees with extreme prejudice. @ Timstuff: You forgot metphysical biologist, physicist, bureaucratic administrator and sex toy.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Fri May 29, 2009 9:37 am Hmm? Well, I always thought of Rei as a "pet project" for Gendo, hehe. I could never imagine random researchers being allowed near her. But that could just be my impression of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by DummyPlug » Fri May 29, 2009 9:55 am It's hard enough to find a person that masters all the areas of his own field. That's why you have different sub fields and when you get your PhD it's usually on a very specific subject. I pictured Ritsuko as a computational biologist (like me! ). But when she starts to play as a physician it gets less believable. And she also seems to know a lot about quantum physics. OR maybe she just pretends to know and nobody questions her knowledge because she's hot.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Fri May 29, 2009 10:45 am I know this is dragging the thread further off-topic, but I am fully credulous of Ritsuko's abilities. She is meant to be one of the smartest people in the world, you know. To give a similar example, one of the surgeons at my hospital is also a PhD (and a prof), software engineer, business owner, pilot, and parent. And all at 36, not much older than Rits. My take is that Ritsuko's research in biological computing requires a very smart person with a very diverse skill set. EDIT: hey, it's on topic now.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Fri May 29, 2009 11:10 am <tangent> Reichu wrote: (I.e., no real "debate" -- the three missing Rei are accounted for; the rest are in the tank and not in any plugs.)

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Fri May 29, 2009 11:29 am Also, people like Leonardo are good examples of polymaths you were really adept when it came to emerging fields. Presumably a lot of the "science" in NGE is really new and unexplored (there is no degree in Reiology), so that's why I imagine Ritsuko is a polymath in Leo's style.

[]:             Quote Post by DummyPlug » Fri May 29, 2009 12:04 pm Well, business owning, piloting and parenting are not really sciences. He has degrees in medicine and software engineering which is amazing, but if you pop in advanced physics and molecular biology plus genetic engineering, all of them at a level where you can actually innovate, it gets a bit more difficult.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Fri May 29, 2009 12:12 pm The point is, you shouldn't doubt that people like Ritsuko actually exist. Schroedinger is a good example of someone who did seminal work in both advanced physics and biology.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Fri May 29, 2009 12:22 pm I gotta say that Ritsuko's cross-disciplinary stuff, to me, seems fully possible and in fact quite likely. My gf is pretty smart but doesn't make it into genius territory, yet she has better mastery of genetics and neuropharmacology than most doctors. I personally know several people who have degrees in combinations like medicine and electrical engineering (one guy I know has a degree in medicine, EE, CS, and ME, and creates biomechanical replicas of the human vocal tract with a team of other scientists for a living -- probably about the same kind of thing as Ritsuko would be doing if the Eva project were not around, given her knowledge). In terms of the quantum mechanics stuff, that's also quite credible as an addition -- I know a number of people who work as systems programmers but argue about stuff like M-theory in their free time; they aren't trained in it, but they can understand the math and they find the concepts possible to grasp. This seems to be a common trend within CS students, and someone who is as smart as Ritsuko can probably pick up a degree in quantum mechanics along with one for psychology. The multidisciplinary thing is actually not as uncommon as you might think. Robert Anton Wilson had a degree in engineering and another in psychology, and what did he do with them? He worked at playboy and wrote novels (one of which, to tie back, was accused by some science journal to not be written by Wilson at all but by a team of quantum physicists writing under the pen name of Robert Anton Wilson). Some relatively mundane sounding jobs require a huge multidisciplinary background -- an arson investigator must have a background in fire science (which involves chemical engineering) and forensics (which involves chemistry, biology, and often a medical degree) as well as criminal justice (which may be paired with a law degree). What's my point? Speciation is for insects. And people trapped in the last century.

[]:             Quote Post by DummyPlug » Fri May 29, 2009 1:00 pm What's my point? Speciation is for insects. And people trapped in the last century.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Fri May 29, 2009 1:33 pm ^ Polymaths tend to make "fundamental and enduring contributions" over a wide range of fields. If everyone were da Vinci in NGE, it would be hard to swallow (since the story purports to be about ordinary people) - but to have one da Vinci (Rits)? Especially at the ground floor of emergent technologies? Not so hard. Beside, just look at Mr. Spock - mathematician, logician, philosopher, engineer, educator, politician...

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Fri May 29, 2009 1:37 pm DummyPlug wrote: What's my point? Speciation is for insects. And people trapped in the last century.

[]:             Quote Post by DummyPlug » Fri May 29, 2009 2:38 pm Which is not particularly uncommon.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Fri May 29, 2009 2:57 pm DummyPlug wrote: Which is not particularly uncommon.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Fri May 29, 2009 2:59 pm McCoy wrote: I'm a doctor, not a bricklayer!

[]:             Quote Post by DummyPlug » Fri May 29, 2009 3:09 pm Nope, quantum mechanics are demonstrable through experiments (science). Metaphysics are unfalsifiable, therefore, unscientific. Theism is a branch of metaphysics.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Fri May 29, 2009 3:11 pm "Metaphysics" literally means "beyond physics" or "outside physics" or "outside the material world". But "meta" can also mean "a response to". i.e. It is a real field in Evangelion, hehe. We are talking about a story with a rather colourful apocalypse, so we're going to assume only the most incredible super geniuses are involved. Completely fucked up super geniuses, but by God, they're the best~ I'm surprised Ritsuko's talents aren't even more expansive. Maybe warm hand-knit sweaters for Rei, or a hilarious robot friend that doubles as a nurse and gives Rei all her shots and medicines. This thread is about Rei, right? Ah, my brain. x_x
